blocks.content: After completing the gaps, practice the dialog with your answers.
Spelling is the act of saying letters individually to form names or words.
Now it's your turn. Practice the dialog and change the names.

blocks.content:
To make simple direct questions in the present, we use "**Do + person + action?**". 
We can ask about actions, routines, and preferences.

blocks.title: Basic Structure
blocks.details: We use DO combined with I, You, They and We

blocks.title: More ways to answer
blocks.explanation: Use a short answer first. Then you can add extra information.
blocks.details: Keep the same answer pattern every time in this part.

cells.text: Yes, I do. I study at night.
No, I don't. I study in the morning.
Yes, I do. I like pop music.

blocks.text:

Yes, I do. I [] in the []
No, I []. I [] in the afternoon.

Do you [] English?
Do you [] music?
Do you [] games?
Do you [] movies?

blocks.title: Listen and Answer
blocks.instruction: In this exercise, you are Sofia. Answer the questions.

script.speaker: Lucas
script.text: Hi Sofia! Do you study English?

script.speaker: Sofia
script.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I study at night.

script.speaker: Sofia
script.text: No, I don't. I don't have time to.

script.speaker: Sofia
script.text: Yes, I do. I like rock.

script.speaker: Lucas
script.text: Cool! Let's go to a show someday...

options.text: Yes, I do. I like reggae.
No, I don't. I like rock.

questions.question: Do you have time to work?
What kind of music do you like?

blocks.content: Let's learn some activities we do on our daily routine.

blocks.title: Match the related items
blocks.instruction: Connect each item on the left to its related item on the right.

blocks.content: We can add time expressions to say **when** or **how often** an activity happens.

blocks.title: Basic Structure
blocks.explanation: Use Do + you + action + time expression?

cells.text: Yes, I do. I study every day.
Do you brush your teeth every night?
Yes, I do. I brush my teeth every night.
Do you exercise in the afternoon?
Yes, I do. I exercise in the afternoon.
No, I don't. I work in the morning.

blocks.title: Make questions with Do you + routine activities + time expressions
blocks.instruction: Example: Do you brush your teeth every day?

questions.question: Write a question for this answer: Yes, I do. I exercise in the morning.
Write a question for this answer: No, I don't. I don't study every day.
Write a question for this answer: Yes, I do. I take a shower in the morning every day.

questionBuilderItems.answerText: Yes, I do. I exercise in the morning.
No, I don't. I don't study every day.
Yes, I do. I take a shower in the morning every day.

blocks.title: Learning routine activities
blocks.content: Ask your teacher routine activities you are curious about! Use "how do I say _____ in English?"
Let's learn how to ask and answer simple questions about yourself.

messages.speaker: Sarah
messages.text: Can I ask you a question? Are you from Brazil?

messages.speaker: Sarah
messages.text: Cool! I am Portuguese. I'm from Portugal.

messages.speaker: Daniel
messages.text: That's nice, I like Cristiano Ronaldo!

messages.speaker: Sarah
messages.text: Haha, I like Pelé! Well, I need to go now... See you around!

cards.backText: I'm from the United States. / I'm American.
cards.shortMeaning: I'm from the United States. / I'm American.

cards.backText: I'm from South Korea. / I'm Korean.
cards.shortMeaning: I'm from South Korea. / I'm Korean.

blocks.objective: Practice the dialog "Knowing People Better". Change the countries and nationalities used.
blocks.tips: Try changing the expressions and the names of the characters.

blocks.title: How to use A or AN
blocks.explanation:
We use a and an before **singular nouns.** 
**A noun** is a word for a person, place, animal, or thing.
blocks.leftContent:
Before a consonant sound.

A **b**ook
A **c**ar
A **c**hair
A **b**ook
A **m**ouse
blocks.rightContent:
Before a vowel sound.

An **a**pple
An **e**gg
An **i**Phone
An **u**mbrella
An **e**raser

blocks.title: Learning new words
blocks.content: Ask your teacher words you are curious about!  Use "how do I say _____ in English?"

To ask simple questions using the verb to be, we use **"Are + person + information?"**.

We can ask about countries, nationalities, professions, age, and personal information.

blocks.title: Basic Structure
blocks.details: In English, nationalities always start with a capital letter. Example: Brazilian, Portuguese, Chilean.

blocks.title: More ways to answer
blocks.explanation: Use a short answer first. Then you can add extra information.
blocks.details: You can answer with extra personal information after the short answer

cells.text: Yes, I am. I am from Brazil
Yes, I am. I'm 18 years old.

blocks.title: About Me
blocks.instruction: Pretend you are Olivia. Answer the questions using her information.

script.speaker: Olivia
script.text: Hi! My name is Olivia. I'm from Spain, and I'm Spanish. I'm 21 years old. I'm an engineer. I love my job, and I draw every weekend. Nice to meet you!

blocks.title: Match the related items
blocks.instruction: Connect each item on the left to its related item on the right.

blocks.content:
We use **How old are you?** to ask someone's age. In English, **we are an age.**
We always say "**I am 20 years old.**"

blocks.title: Basic Structure
blocks.details: Never use "have" to say your age.

blocks.title: Create Your Identity
blocks.objective: Create a new identity using a different name, country, nationality, age, and profession.

customFields.label: STUDENT A
customFields.value:
**Interview Student B:**
What's your name?
Where are you from?
What's your nationality?
What do you do?
How old are you?

customFields.label: STUDENT B
customFields.value:
**Answer the questions using:**
A different name.
A different country.
A different nationality.
A different profession.
A different age.

blocks.content: Review everything you learned this lesson in this page.

messages.speaker: Jacob
messages.text: Hi! Nice to meet you. I'm Jacob.

messages.speaker: Nico
messages.text: Pleased to meet you too, Jacob. I'm Brazilian. Are you from Brazil?

messages.speaker: Jacob
messages.text: No, I'm not. I'm Korean. I'm a teacher.

messages.speaker: Nico
messages.text: Yes, I do. I study English and engineering.

messages.speaker: Jacob
messages.text: That's great! I need to go now. See you around!

blocks.title: A Happy Boy
blocks.instruction: Pretend you are Noah. Answer the questions using his information.

script.speaker: NOAH
script.text: Hello! I'm Noah. I'm from the United States. I'm American. I'm 16 years old. I'm a baker. I study Spanish every day. Nice to meet you!

blocks.title: Knowing You Better
blocks.prompt:
Write a short paragraph about yourself.
Say a greeting, your name, your age, your profession, what you do, your country, and your nationality.
